name: pyecharts (Apache ECharts for Python)

performance.notes: Generates HTML/JS; rendering in browser; Python-side is fast

agent_readiness.notes: Apache ECharts binding for Python — rich interactive charts. from pyecharts.charts import Bar, Line, Pie, Scatter, Map, Geo, Graph, Gauge, Sankey, Sunburst, TreeMap, WordCloud, Funnel, Radar, Boxplot. from pyecharts import options as opts. bar = Bar(). bar.add_xaxis(['Jan', 'Feb', 'Mar']). bar.add_yaxis('Sales', [100, 200, 150]). bar.set_global_opts(title_opts=opts.TitleOpts(title='Monthly Sales')). bar.render('chart.html'). Jupyter: bar.render_notebook(). Grid: from pyecharts.charts import Grid. Composite charts: Tab, Page, Timeline. Chinese docs primary — English docs available but may lag. Globe: pyecharts-extras for 3D globe.

gotchas: Primary documentation is Chinese — English docs may be outdated or incomplete
Large number of options — steep learning curve for complex charts
No type annotations — poor IDE support for opts configuration
ECharts JS version pinned — may lag behind ECharts releases
render_notebook() requires special Jupyter config; may not work in all environments
best_when: Rich interactive charts with ECharts quality (beyond plotly's defaults)
Specialized chart types: Sankey, Sunburst, TreeMap, Globe, Geo choropleth
Chinese-language projects where ECharts is the de-facto standard
Static HTML chart export for embedding in web pages
avoid_when: When plotly or bokeh is sufficient for interactive charts
Static publication-quality charts (use matplotlib or seaborn)
When English documentation is critical for your team
summary: pyecharts binds Apache ECharts to Python. Bar().add_xaxis().add_yaxis().render('chart.html'). Rich chart types: Sankey, Sunburst, Globe, Geo, WordCloud, TreeMap. Jupyter support. Tab/Page/Timeline for composites. MIT by pyecharts team. Best for ECharts-quality interactive charts. Chinese docs primary. No type annotations. Large option space. Use plotly/bokeh for simpler interactive needs.
